chunks.text:
FINTRAC suspicious transaction guidance placeholder

This local-first demo file is a placeholder for manually added authoritative guidance.

Analysts should review unusual cross-border activity, abrupt increases in transaction amount relative to normal customer behavior, rapid transaction velocity, new-device activity, and unexplained first-time merchant usage.

Location mismatch on its own may be weak evidence when customer travel is already known. It should be weighed together with device familiarity, merchant history, prior spending pattern, and any analyst notes.

chunks.text:
Location mismatch on its own may be weak evidence when customer travel is already known. It should be weighed together with device familiarity, merchant history, prior spending pattern, and any analyst notes.

Case notes should record why a transaction appears suspicious, which signals raised concern, what context reduced concern, and why escalation or closure was recommended.

Replace or extend this file only with guidance content that you are allowed to store locally for the demo.

Location mismatch on its own may be weak evidence when customer travel is already known. It should be weighed together with device familiarity, merchant history, prior spending pattern, and any analyst notes.

chunks.text:
Electronics and luxury-style purchases
Electronics and other high-resale categories often deserve closer review when paired with first-use device activity, cross-border spending, or unusually high amounts.

Travel and lodging merchants
Travel merchants may represent valid customer behavior, especially when travel notice exists, but they still require review when a new device and high amount appear together.

Context-first interpretation
Merchant category alone is not a fraud outcome. Analysts should combine category, device familiarity, country mismatch, and transaction velocity before escalating.

Merchant Risk Reference
Merchant category review cues
Demo
merchant electronics travel cross-border high-amount
Electronics and luxury-style purchases
Electronics and other high-resale categories often deserve closer review when paired with first-use device activity, cross-border spending, or unusually high amounts.

Travel and lodging merchants
Travel merchants may represent valid customer behavior, especially when travel notice exists, but they still require review when a new device and high amount appear together.

Context-first interpretation
Merchant category alone is not a fraud outcome. Analysts should combine category, device familiarity, country mismatch, and transaction velocity before escalating.

chunks.text:
Travel context review checklist for the demo:

If travel notice is on file, analysts should verify whether the location change aligns with the declared travel window before treating location mismatch as a strong signal.

Known device plus travel notice usually reduces concern unless there is also unusual amount growth, rapid repeat attempts, or a merchant pattern that conflicts with recent customer behavior.

New device plus foreign merchant plus elevated amount should still receive closer review even when travel is plausible.

Analyst notes should state whether travel context was verified, assumed, missing, or contradicted by other evidence.

Travel context review checklist for the demo:

If travel notice is on file, analysts should verify whether the location change aligns with the declared travel window before treating location mismatch as a strong signal.

Known device plus travel notice usually reduces concern unless there is also unusual amount growth, rapid repeat attempts, or a merchant pattern that conflicts with recent customer behavior.

New device plus foreign merchant plus elevated amount should still receive closer review even when travel is plausible.

Analyst notes should state whether travel context was verified, assumed, missing, or contradicted by other evidence.
